data_in.description: Custom feature list artifact (CSV or TSV format) containing m/z, retention time, and intensity columns
MS2 spectral data (mzML or equivalent) for fragment-based diagnostic filtering

data_out.description: Prioritized feature table (CSV) containing custom input features annotated with MD/C scores, KMD values, and MS2 fragment match status
Filter validation report (CSV or JSON) showing per-feature pass/fail status for each prioritization criterion (MD/C threshold, KMD clustering, MS2 match)

domain_knowledge: MD/C (mass defect / molecular mass ratio) is a key discriminator for PFAS identification because fluorine incorporation creates characteristic mass defects that differentiate PFAS from isomeric non-fluorinated compounds.

Kendrick mass defect analysis clusters PFAS homologues by molecular weight spacing (typically 2 × 13.007825 Da per CF₂ unit) and is most effective when features share a common fluorinated backbone structure.

Custom feature lists bypass automated pyOpenMS peak detection and must supply exact m/z values, retention times, and intensities to ensure compatibility with downstream mass-based filters that assume feature-level precision.

MS2 diagnostic fragments (e.g., CF₃⁻, CF₂⁻, or SO₂NH⁻ for PFOS-like compounds) are structure-specific and their presence/absence in the MS2 data is used to confirm or reject candidate PFAS features identified by mass-based filters alone.

verify pipeline accepts CSV or TSV artifact as optional custom feature list input
verify custom feature list input does not trigger pyOpenMS feature detection when supplied
script_runs: execute pipeline with custom CSV feature list artifact and verify no errors or fallback to automated detection
verify downstream MD/C filter reads and processes custom feature list records without modification to filter logic
verify downstream KMD filter reads and processes custom feature list records without modification to filter logic
verify downstream MS2 fragment filter reads and processes custom feature list records without modification to filter logic
verify output of prioritization filters applied to custom feature data matches expected schema (any of: feature ID, m/z, retention time, prioritization score, filter pass/fail labels)
expert_review: parameter-sensitive — confirm that MD/C, KMD, and MS2 thresholds are applied identically whether features originate from pyOpenMS detection or custom CSV supply
expert_review: Review whether custom feature list format specification (column names, data types, required vs. optional fields) is documented or inferred from code
Assess whether edge cases (missing m/z, missing MS2 data, malformed rows) are handled gracefully and do not silently corrupt downstream filter logic
Evaluate whether any pyOpenMS-specific metadata (e.g. feature quality scores, detection confidence) is required or optional when using custom feature input
Judge whether the prioritization filters produce chemically and statistically defensible PFAS rankings when applied to externally supplied feature data

Filter validation report (CSV or JSON) showing per-feature pass/fail status for each prioritization criterion (MD/C threshold, KMD clustering, MS2 match)
finding: PFΔScreen supports optional inclusion of custom feature lists as an alternative input pathway, and applies prioritization techniques including MD/C-m/C approach, KMD analysis, and MS2 fragment analysis to features regardless of their source.
inputs: Custom feature list artifact (CSV or TSV format) containing m/z, retention time, and intensity columns
MS2 spectral data (mzML or equivalent) for fragment-based diagnostic filtering
methodology_summary: Parse and validate custom feature list CSV/TSV, confirming presence of required columns (m/z, retention time, intensity) and numeric data types.
Convert custom feature objects into PFΔScreen's internal feature representation to ensure compatibility with downstream filter modules.
Calculate mass defect and mass defect / nominal mass ratio (MD/C) for each custom feature; flag features exceeding PFAS-typical MD/C thresholds.
Perform Kendrick mass defect clustering to identify homologous series within the custom feature set.
Match each custom feature m/z against MS2 fragmentation patterns to detect diagnostic PFAS fragments and confirm structural hypotheses.

Validation: Verify that at least 90% of custom features receive a prioritization score and that filter pass/fail status can be independently audited against reference PFAS standards or known-mass spike compounds.

task_description: Extend PFΔScreen's input pipeline to accept an optional custom feature list (CSV/TSV format) as an alternative to pyOpenMS-detected features, and validate that the downstream PFAS prioritization filters (MD/C, KMD, and MS2 fragment analysis) operate correctly on externally supplied feature data.
task_objective: Implement and validate custom feature list import (CSV/TSV) in PFΔScreen and confirm all prioritization filters operate identically on externally supplied versus pyOpenMS-detected features.

task_outputs.label: Prioritized feature table with MD/C, KMD, and MS2 annotations

title: Extend PFΔScreen prioritization by incorporating a user-supplied custom feature list

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: Filter validation report (CSV or JSON) showing per-feature pass/fail status for each prioritization criterion (MD/C threshold, KMD clustering, MS2 match).
workflow_description: 1. Load custom feature list from user-supplied CSV/TSV file, validating required columns (m/z, retention time, intensity). 2. Parse feature metadata and convert to internal PFΔScreen feature object format compatible with downstream prioritization modules. 3. Apply MD/C-m/C ratio filtering to identify PFAS-like features with elevated mass defects. 4. Perform Kendrick mass defect (KMD) analysis on filtered features to cluster potential PFAS homologues. 5. Cross-reference features against MS2 fragment data to detect diagnostic fragment mass differences indicative of PFAS structure. 6. Generate prioritized feature table and comparison report showing which custom features pass each prioritization filter.
